_README: Ecosystem-shared parameterization registry — the committed schema TEMPLATE.
Resolved through .claude/bin/lib/ecosystem-config.mjs + the WHICH-layer join
in loom-links.mjs (resolveRepo / resolveRemote).
PURPOSE (D6 keystone — specs/03 §3)
loom-links.local.json answers WHERE-on-disk a logical key is (per-operator,
gitignored). THIS file answers the orthogonal ECOSYSTEM-level questions that
are the SAME for every operator in one ecosystem but DIFFER across ecosystems
(canon vs a client fork): which container registry, which remote {org,repo}
each logical key points at, the VCS provider defaults/overrides, the deploy
targets, and the upstream-canon this ecosystem syncs from.
THE REAL FILE IS ecosystem.json (NOT this .example). It is COMMITTED-and-shared
WITHIN one ecosystem but fenced THREE ways so it never crosses an ecosystem
boundary and never reaches the public fork:
- never synced            (sync-manifest.yaml loom_only:)
- never published         (scripts/publish-to-public.mjs EXCLUDE_WITHIN + KILL)
- never scanned-as-content (scan-synced-disclosure.mjs ecosystem.json exclude)
Each fork AUTHORS ITS OWN ecosystem.json at fork time (it never inherits canon's
— that is the structural basis of cross-ecosystem disclosure isolation). THIS
.example carries SYNTHETIC tokens only and is the only ecosystem* file the public
fork carries.
BACK-COMPAT
The ABSENCE of ecosystem.json is NOT an error: a consumer (which never receives
it) sees every accessor return null/its default and loom-links resolution is
byte-identical to today. A PRESENT-but-malformed / unknown schema_version file
fails LOUD — never silently read as v1.
FIELDS
schema_version          MUST equal the loom's SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION (1).
ecosystem.id            opaque LOCAL label (e.g. "canon") — NOT a client name.
ecosystem.upstream_canon {remote,url} the 'sync upstream from' pointer; null in
canon (canon is the root); a fork names its canon.
registry                {host,org} → composes ${host}/${org}/<image>.
remote_links            logical key → {org,repo}. Keys are EXACTLY the resolver
keys: build.{py,rs,prism}, use-template.{...}, loom,
atelier, downstream.<slug>. Local owns WHERE; this owns
WHICH; resolveRepo joins them.
vcs.default_provider    ecosystem default ('github' | 'azure-devops').
vcs.overrides           per-key provider override (roster.genesis.provider for
a repo's OWN provider still wins over this).
vcs.ado_work_item_type  ADO work-item type (G-F upflow); default 'Task'.
deploy                  {default_targets,per_project} — ecosystem-aware deploy.
